title: DeMiAn: How to Instruct Your Robot: Dense Language Annotations Power Robot Policy Learning
summary: DeMiAn (Dense Multi-Aspect Annotation) enriches robot training by re-annotating existing video demonstrations with detailed language descriptions generated by a vision-language model across four aspects: physical motion, scene composition, arm pose, and reasoning. A learned instructor model maps a task description and initial scene snapshot to a task-appropriate annotation at deployment, running asynchronously to hide generation latency behind policy execution. Evaluated across over 1M robot manipulation clips and 50K human-egocentric videos, it improves both a VLA policy and a video-based world-action model without collecting new demonstrations.
